summarization.a: XPS Ti 2p region showing signals of TiO2 and Ru for conventionally grown TiO2.
summarization.b: XPS O 1s region showing signals of TiO2 and adsorbed O2 for conventionally grown TiO2.
summarization.c: XPS Ti 2p region showing signals of TiO2 and Ru for TiO2 grown with H2O-added sequence.
summarization.d: XPS O 1s region showing signals of TiO2 and adsorbed O2 for TiO2 grown with H2O-added sequence.

data_extraction.a:
| Species | Binding energy [eV] |
|---|---|
| TiO2 | Peaks at 458 and 464 |
| Ru | Peak at 460 |
data_extraction.b:

|---|---|
| TiO2 | Peak at 529 |
| Abs. O2 | Peak at 531 |

| Species | Binding energy [eV] |
|---|---|
| TiO2 | Peaks at 458 and 464 |

|---|---|

a.question: Where are the typical Ti(IV) peaks found in the Ti 2p region?
By comparing a) to c) and b) to d), what can be derived from the properties of the two different films?
a.answer: When comparing these two sets of XPS spectra, it becomes apparent that they are practically identical. The chemical environment of both titanium and oxygen species in both films are therefore highly similar, meaning that using H2O in the ALD process did not lead to a TiO2 film with significantly different properties.

b.question: Where is the typical oxygen peak for TiO2 found in the O 1s region?
What does "Abs." refer to?
b.answer: Oxygen components other than TiO2 that were absorbed in the ALD-grown layer during the synthesis.
